mnemonics: GATES CONTROL TRAFFIC: Think of Gated RNNs using Forget, Input, and Output gates to control the information traffic (memory flow) like a traffic controller.
keyPoints: Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs) use an update gate and a reset gate to control information flow.
Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks use input, forget, and output gates to manage their cell state.
The gating mechanisms solve the vanishing gradient problem, allowing the network to learn long-range dependencies.
commonMistakes: Confusing the roles of the LSTM gates (e.g., thinking the forget gate adds information).
Assuming GRUs are always faster and better than LSTMs; the performance is highly task-dependent.
Forgetting to apply the cell state activation function (usually tanh) before the output gate in an LSTM.
